speaker: ROBIN
text: How now, spirit? Whither wander you?

speaker: FAIRY
text: Over hill, over dale,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Thorough bush, thorough brier,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Over park, over pale,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Thorough flood, thorough fire;

speaker: FAIRY
text: I do wander everywhere,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Swifter than the moon’s sphere.

speaker: FAIRY
text: And I serve the Fairy Queen,

speaker: FAIRY
text: To dew her orbs upon the green.

speaker: FAIRY
text: The cowslips tall her pensioners be;

speaker: FAIRY
text: In their gold coats spots you see;

speaker: FAIRY
text: Those be rubies, fairy favors;

speaker: FAIRY
text: In those freckles live their savors.

speaker: FAIRY
text: I must go seek some dewdrops here

speaker: FAIRY
text: And hang a pearl in every cowslip’s ear.

speaker: FAIRY
text: Farewell, thou lob of spirits. I’ll be gone.

speaker: FAIRY
text: Our queen and all her elves come here anon.

speaker: ROBIN
text: The King doth keep his revels here tonight.

speaker: ROBIN
text: Take heed the Queen come not within his sight,

speaker: ROBIN
text: For Oberon is passing fell and wrath

speaker: ROBIN
text: Because that she, as her attendant, hath

speaker: ROBIN
text: A lovely boy stolen from an Indian king;

speaker: ROBIN
text: She never had so sweet a changeling.

speaker: ROBIN
text: And jealous Oberon would have the child

speaker: ROBIN
text: Knight of his train, to trace the forests wild.

speaker: ROBIN
text: But she perforce withholds the lovèd boy,

speaker: ROBIN
text: Crowns him with flowers and makes him all her joy.

speaker: ROBIN
text: And now they never meet in grove or green,

speaker: ROBIN
text: By fountain clear or spangled starlight sheen,

speaker: ROBIN
text: But they do square, that all their elves for fear

speaker: ROBIN
text: Creep into acorn cups and hide them there.

speaker: FAIRY
text: Either I mistake your shape and making quite,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Or else you are that shrewd and knavish sprite

speaker: FAIRY
text: Called Robin Goodfellow. Are not you he

speaker: FAIRY
text: That frights the maidens of the villagery,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Skim milk, and sometimes labor in the quern

speaker: FAIRY
text: And bootless make the breathless huswife churn,

speaker: FAIRY
text: And sometime make the drink to bear no barm,

speaker: FAIRY
text: Mislead night wanderers, laughing at their harm?

speaker: FAIRY
text: Those that “Hobgoblin” call you and “sweet Puck,”

speaker: FAIRY
text: You do their work, and they shall have good luck.

speaker: FAIRY
text: Are not you he?

speaker: ROBIN
text: Thou speakest aright.

speaker: ROBIN
text: I am that merry wanderer of the night.

speaker: ROBIN
text: I jest to Oberon and make him smile

speaker: ROBIN
text: When I a fat and bean-fed horse beguile,

speaker: ROBIN
text: Neighing in likeness of a filly foal.

speaker: ROBIN
text: And sometime lurk I in a gossip’s bowl

speaker: ROBIN
text: In very likeness of a roasted crab,

speaker: ROBIN
text: And, when she drinks, against her lips I bob

speaker: ROBIN
text: And on her withered dewlap pour the ale.

speaker: ROBIN
text: The wisest aunt, telling the saddest tale,

speaker: ROBIN
text: Sometime for three-foot stool mistaketh me;

speaker: ROBIN
text: Then slip I from her bum, down topples she

speaker: ROBIN
text: And “Tailor!” cries and falls into a cough,

speaker: ROBIN
text: And then the whole choir hold their hips and loffe

speaker: ROBIN
text: And waxen in their mirth and neeze and swear

speaker: ROBIN
text: A merrier hour was never wasted there.

speaker: ROBIN
text: But room, fairy. Here comes Oberon.

speaker: FAIRY
text: And here my mistress. Would that he were gone!

speaker: OBERON
text: Ill met by moonlight, proud Titania.

speaker: TITANIA
text: What, jealous Oberon? Fairies, skip hence.

speaker: TITANIA
text: I have forsworn his bed and company.

speaker: OBERON
text: Tarry, rash wanton. Am not I thy lord?

speaker: TITANIA
text: Then I must be thy lady. But I know

speaker: TITANIA
text: When thou hast stolen away from Fairyland

speaker: TITANIA
text: And in the shape of Corin sat all day

speaker: TITANIA
text: Playing on pipes of corn and versing love

speaker: TITANIA
text: To amorous Phillida. Why art thou here,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Come from the farthest steep of India,

speaker: TITANIA
text: But that, forsooth, the bouncing Amazon,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Your buskined mistress and your warrior love,

speaker: TITANIA
text: To Theseus must be wedded, and you come

speaker: TITANIA
text: To give their bed joy and prosperity?

speaker: OBERON
text: How canst thou thus for shame, Titania,

speaker: OBERON
text: Glance at my credit with Hippolyta,

speaker: OBERON
text: Knowing I know thy love to Theseus?

speaker: OBERON
text: Didst not thou lead him through the glimmering night

speaker: OBERON
text: From Perigouna, whom he ravishèd,

speaker: OBERON
text: And make him with fair Aegles break his faith,

speaker: OBERON
text: With Ariadne and Antiopa?

speaker: TITANIA
text: These are the forgeries of jealousy;

speaker: TITANIA
text: And never, since the middle summer’s spring,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Met we on hill, in dale, forest, or mead,

speaker: TITANIA
text: By pavèd fountain or by rushy brook,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Or in the beachèd margent of the sea,

speaker: TITANIA
text: To dance our ringlets to the whistling wind,

speaker: TITANIA
text: But with thy brawls thou hast disturbed our sport.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Therefore the winds, piping to us in vain,

speaker: TITANIA
text: As in revenge have sucked up from the sea

speaker: TITANIA
text: Contagious fogs, which, falling in the land,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Hath every pelting river made so proud

speaker: TITANIA
text: That they have overborne their continents.

speaker: TITANIA
text: The ox hath therefore stretched his yoke in vain,

speaker: TITANIA
text: The plowman lost his sweat, and the green corn

speaker: TITANIA
text: Hath rotted ere his youth attained a beard.

speaker: TITANIA
text: The fold stands empty in the drownèd field,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And crows are fatted with the murrain flock.

speaker: TITANIA
text: The nine-men’s-morris is filled up with mud,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And the quaint mazes in the wanton green,

speaker: TITANIA
text: For lack of tread, are undistinguishable.

speaker: TITANIA
text: The human mortals want their winter here.

speaker: TITANIA
text: No night is now with hymn or carol blessed.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Therefore the moon, the governess of floods,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Pale in her anger, washes all the air,

speaker: TITANIA
text: That rheumatic diseases do abound.

speaker: TITANIA
text: And thorough this distemperature we see

speaker: TITANIA
text: The seasons alter: hoary-headed frosts

speaker: TITANIA
text: Fall in the fresh lap of the crimson rose,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And on old Hiems’ thin and icy crown

speaker: TITANIA
text: An odorous chaplet of sweet summer buds

speaker: TITANIA
text: Is, as in mockery, set. The spring, the summer,

speaker: TITANIA
text: The childing autumn, angry winter, change

speaker: TITANIA
text: Their wonted liveries, and the mazèd world

speaker: TITANIA
text: By their increase now knows not which is which.

speaker: TITANIA
text: And this same progeny of evils comes

speaker: TITANIA
text: From our debate, from our dissension;

speaker: TITANIA
text: We are their parents and original.

speaker: OBERON
text: Do you amend it, then. It lies in you.

speaker: OBERON
text: Why should Titania cross her Oberon?

speaker: OBERON
text: I do but beg a little changeling boy

speaker: OBERON
text: To be my henchman.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Set your heart at rest:

speaker: TITANIA
text: The Fairyland buys not the child of me.

speaker: TITANIA
text: His mother was a vot’ress of my order,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And in the spicèd Indian air by night

speaker: TITANIA
text: Full often hath she gossiped by my side

speaker: TITANIA
text: And sat with me on Neptune’s yellow sands,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Marking th’ embarkèd traders on the flood,

speaker: TITANIA
text: When we have laughed to see the sails conceive

speaker: TITANIA
text: And grow big-bellied with the wanton wind;

speaker: TITANIA
text: Which she, with pretty and with swimming gait,

speaker: TITANIA
text: Following (her womb then rich with my young squire),

speaker: TITANIA
text: Would imitate and sail upon the land

speaker: TITANIA
text: To fetch me trifles and return again,

speaker: TITANIA
text: As from a voyage, rich with merchandise.

speaker: TITANIA
text: But she, being mortal, of that boy did die,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And for her sake do I rear up her boy,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And for her sake I will not part with him.

speaker: OBERON
text: How long within this wood intend you stay?

speaker: TITANIA
text: Perchance till after Theseus’ wedding day.

speaker: TITANIA
text: If you will patiently dance in our round

speaker: TITANIA
text: And see our moonlight revels, go with us.

speaker: TITANIA
text: If not, shun me, and I will spare your haunts.

speaker: OBERON
text: Give me that boy and I will go with thee.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Not for thy fairy kingdom. Fairies, away.

speaker: TITANIA
text: We shall chide downright if I longer stay.

speaker: OBERON
text: Well, go thy way. Thou shalt not from this grove

speaker: OBERON
text: Till I torment thee for this injury.—

speaker: OBERON
text: My gentle Puck, come hither. Thou rememb’rest

speaker: OBERON
text: Since once I sat upon a promontory

speaker: OBERON
text: And heard a mermaid on a dolphin’s back

speaker: OBERON
text: Uttering such dulcet and harmonious breath

speaker: OBERON
text: That the rude sea grew civil at her song

speaker: OBERON
text: And certain stars shot madly from their spheres

speaker: OBERON
text: To hear the sea-maid’s music.

speaker: ROBIN
text: I remember.

speaker: OBERON
text: That very time I saw (but thou couldst not),

speaker: OBERON
text: Flying between the cold moon and the Earth,

speaker: OBERON
text: Cupid all armed. A certain aim he took

speaker: OBERON
text: At a fair vestal thronèd by the west,

speaker: OBERON
text: And loosed his love-shaft smartly from his bow

speaker: OBERON
text: As it should pierce a hundred thousand hearts.

speaker: OBERON
text: But I might see young Cupid’s fiery shaft

speaker: OBERON
text: Quenched in the chaste beams of the wat’ry moon,

speaker: OBERON
text: And the imperial vot’ress passèd on

speaker: OBERON
text: In maiden meditation, fancy-free.

speaker: OBERON
text: Yet marked I where the bolt of Cupid fell.

speaker: OBERON
text: It fell upon a little western flower,

speaker: OBERON
text: Before, milk-white, now purple with love’s wound,

speaker: OBERON
text: And maidens call it “love-in-idleness.”

speaker: OBERON
text: Fetch me that flower; the herb I showed thee once.

speaker: OBERON
text: The juice of it on sleeping eyelids laid

speaker: OBERON
text: Will make or man or woman madly dote

speaker: OBERON
text: Upon the next live creature that it sees.

speaker: OBERON
text: Fetch me this herb, and be thou here again

speaker: OBERON
text: Ere the leviathan can swim a league.

speaker: ROBIN
text: I’ll put a girdle round about the Earth

speaker: ROBIN
text: In forty minutes.

speaker: OBERON
text: Having once this juice,

speaker: OBERON
text: I’ll watch Titania when she is asleep

speaker: OBERON
text: And drop the liquor of it in her eyes.

speaker: OBERON
text: The next thing then she, waking, looks upon

speaker: OBERON
text: (Be it on lion, bear, or wolf, or bull,

speaker: OBERON
text: On meddling monkey, or on busy ape)

speaker: OBERON
text: She shall pursue it with the soul of love.

speaker: OBERON
text: And ere I take this charm from off her sight

speaker: OBERON
text: (As I can take it with another herb),

speaker: OBERON
text: I’ll make her render up her page to me.

speaker: OBERON
text: But who comes here? I am invisible,

speaker: OBERON
text: And I will overhear their conference.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I love thee not; therefore pursue me not.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Where is Lysander and fair Hermia?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: The one I’ll stay; the other stayeth me.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Thou told’st me they were stol’n unto this wood,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And here am I, and wood within this wood

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Because I cannot meet my Hermia.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Hence, get thee gone, and follow me no more.

speaker: HELENA
text: You draw me, you hard-hearted adamant!

speaker: HELENA
text: But yet you draw not iron, for my heart

speaker: HELENA
text: Is true as steel. Leave you your power to draw,

speaker: HELENA
text: And I shall have no power to follow you.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Do I entice you? Do I speak you fair?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Or rather do I not in plainest truth

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Tell you I do not, nor I cannot love you?

speaker: HELENA
text: And even for that do I love you the more.

speaker: HELENA
text: I am your spaniel, and, Demetrius,

speaker: HELENA
text: The more you beat me I will fawn on you.

speaker: HELENA
text: Use me but as your spaniel: spurn me, strike me,

speaker: HELENA
text: Neglect me, lose me; only give me leave

speaker: HELENA
text: (Unworthy as I am) to follow you.

speaker: HELENA
text: What worser place can I beg in your love

speaker: HELENA
text: (And yet a place of high respect with me)

speaker: HELENA
text: Than to be usèd as you use your dog?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Tempt not too much the hatred of my spirit,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: For I am sick when I do look on thee.

speaker: HELENA
text: And I am sick when I look not on you.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: You do impeach your modesty too much

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: To leave the city and commit yourself

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Into the hands of one that loves you not,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: To trust the opportunity of night

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And the ill counsel of a desert place

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: With the rich worth of your virginity.

speaker: HELENA
text: Your virtue is my privilege. For that

speaker: HELENA
text: It is not night when I do see your face,

speaker: HELENA
text: Therefore I think I am not in the night.

speaker: HELENA
text: Nor doth this wood lack worlds of company,

speaker: HELENA
text: For you, in my respect, are all the world.

speaker: HELENA
text: Then, how can it be said I am alone

speaker: HELENA
text: When all the world is here to look on me?

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I’ll run from thee and hide me in the brakes

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And leave thee to the mercy of wild beasts.

speaker: HELENA
text: The wildest hath not such a heart as you.

speaker: HELENA
text: Run when you will. The story shall be changed:

speaker: HELENA
text: Apollo flies and Daphne holds the chase;

speaker: HELENA
text: The dove pursues the griffin; the mild hind

speaker: HELENA
text: Makes speed to catch the tiger. Bootless speed

speaker: HELENA
text: When cowardice pursues and valor flies!

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: I will not stay thy questions. Let me go,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Or if thou follow me, do not believe

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: But I shall do thee mischief in the wood.

speaker: HELENA
text: Ay, in the temple, in the town, the field,

speaker: HELENA
text: You do me mischief. Fie, Demetrius!

speaker: HELENA
text: Your wrongs do set a scandal on my sex.

speaker: HELENA
text: We cannot fight for love as men may do.

speaker: HELENA
text: We should be wooed and were not made to woo.

speaker: HELENA
text: I’ll follow thee and make a heaven of hell

speaker: HELENA
text: To die upon the hand I love so well.

speaker: OBERON
text: Fare thee well, nymph. Ere he do leave this grove,

speaker: OBERON
text: Thou shalt fly him, and he shall seek thy love.

speaker: OBERON
text: Hast thou the flower there? Welcome, wanderer.

speaker: ROBIN
text: Ay, there it is.

speaker: OBERON
text: I pray thee give it me.

speaker: OBERON
text: I know a bank where the wild thyme blows,

speaker: OBERON
text: Where oxlips and the nodding violet grows,

speaker: OBERON
text: Quite overcanopied with luscious woodbine,

speaker: OBERON
text: With sweet muskroses, and with eglantine.

speaker: OBERON
text: There sleeps Titania sometime of the night,

speaker: OBERON
text: Lulled in these flowers with dances and delight.

speaker: OBERON
text: And there the snake throws her enameled skin,

speaker: OBERON
text: Weed wide enough to wrap a fairy in.

speaker: OBERON
text: And with the juice of this I’ll streak her eyes

speaker: OBERON
text: And make her full of hateful fantasies.

speaker: OBERON
text: Take thou some of it, and seek through this grove.

speaker: OBERON
text: A sweet Athenian lady is in love

speaker: OBERON
text: With a disdainful youth. Anoint his eyes,

speaker: OBERON
text: But do it when the next thing he espies

speaker: OBERON
text: May be the lady. Thou shalt know the man

speaker: OBERON
text: By the Athenian garments he hath on.

speaker: OBERON
text: Effect it with some care, that he may prove

speaker: OBERON
text: More fond on her than she upon her love.

speaker: OBERON
text: And look thou meet me ere the first cock crow.

speaker: ROBIN
text: Fear not, my lord. Your servant shall do so.
